Recommendation:

1.6	We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries to accurately reflect the correct balances in the affected accounts, ensuring the financial statements are accurate and compliant with accounting standards.

Recommendations:
We recommended and it was agreed that Management:
2.14.1	Instruct the Municipal Accountant to record the required journal entries to reclassify tangible items, with costs below the capitalization threshold and already issued to end-users, that remain in the PPE account, in accordance with the illustrative entries outlined in Annex C of COA Circular No. 2024-06 dated March 14, 2024;

Prior period adjustment 			xxx
Other Supplies and Materials Inventory		xxx
Strictly comply with Sections 4.17 to 4.19 of COA Circular 2022-04 regarding the proper documentation, reporting, and accountability of lost or missing semi-expendable properties.

Recommendation:

3.11	We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to adjust the Accounts Payable balance by recognizing the unreported obligation and the corresponding asset, ensuring that the financial statements accurately reflect the municipality's financial position and comply with relevant accounting standards.

Recommendations:

12.10	We recommended and Management concurred to:
12.10.1	Evaluate the LGU’s current organizational structure and staffing levels of the LGU, particularly within the Accounting and Treasury Offices, and consider increasing personnel to better address the existing workload. Also, department heads should facilitate or organize orientation and training sessions for employees and staff assigned to these units, ensuring that they are adequately equipped with the knowledge and skills necessary to perform their duties effectively;
12.10.2	Create a team to expedite the submission of delayed accounts for the period October to December 2024, ensuring that all Disbursement Vouchers are submitted, accompanied by complete and accurate supporting documents, not later than February 28, 2025, to prevent the issuance of a Notice of Suspension; and
12.10.3	Strictly observe the reglementary periods set for the submission of accounts and reports for a prompt audit and early reporting of audit results.

Recommendations:

14.11 We recommended and Management is agreeable to promptly undertake a comprehensive evaluation of its procurement procedures, ensuring strict compliance with the guidelines set forth in RA 9184 and its IRR. Also, augment employee/s to help the BAC Secretariat carry-out its mandated functions.

We recommended that Management:

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant comply with COA Circular 2016-005, and prepare the necessary request for write-off of dormant asset accounts.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconciles the balances of the reciprocal accounts under the three Funds and book the necessary adjustments to bring their balances into agreement. Also, prepare and file a request for write off for those long dormant accounts as prescribed in COA Circular 2016-005. Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant should closely monitor transactions affecting reciprocal accounts and ensure that these are recorded in the different funds simultaneously to avoid any error of omission.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary journal entries to record the above-mentioned properties in the books of the agency pursuant to the provisions of the abovementioned Government Accounting Manual.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reclassify the completed project to the appropriate asset account.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepares the necessary adjusting entries to recognize the above-mentioned properties in the books of accounts.
 
We also recommended that assessment should be made on the donated property to come up with its fair market value as basis for its recording in the books of accounts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the GSO to prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Properties (IIRUP) and institute measures to properly dispose all obsolete and unserviceable properties pursuant to the directives of Section 79 of PD 1445 and Sections 379-381 of RA 7160.
 
We also recommended that the original copy of the IIRUP should be given to the Municipal Accountant as basis for making the necessary adjusting entries in dropping the said unserviceable properties from the books of accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the actual accomplishments and correct amounts of all the on-going infrastructure projects.
 
We also recommended that the Municipal Engineer should monitor monthly or quarterly all the on-going projects and inform the Municipal Accountant of their status so that actual accomplishments and amounts will be recorded in the books of accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Health Officer and his staff to use the biometric machine and require them to have complete entries in the DTR showing their arrival and departure in the morning and in the afternoon (biometric generated). If there are no entries in the DTR, approved Travel Orders and Certificates of Appearance should be attached.  

Require the Municipal Accountant not to process the payment of their hazard allowance if their DTRs are not properly accomplished and the required and approved supporting documents were not `attached. 

Further, we recommended that a separate voucher be prepared for the payment of hazard and strict compliance of the documentary requirements be observed.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to stop the grant of hazard pay unless the above-cited regulations have been followed and its supporting documents have been submitted.

Require the Municipal Accountant to process the payment of hazard after the requirements mentioned above have been submitted to avoid the issuance of disallowance.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor not to grant subsistence allowance to Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer and Public Health Officers who are not rendering their services specified in certain provision of DBM-DSWD Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2018 and Sec. 22 of Republic Act 7305, The Magna Carta of Public Health Workers.

Require the Municipal Accountant not to process the payment of subsistence allowance if the supporting documents pertaining thereto are not complete and to prepare a separate disbursement voucher for its payment so that   compliance of its requirements can be strictly observed. 

Also, require the persons concerned to use the biometric machine and entries in the DTR should be complete showing their arrival and departure in the morning and afternoon.  Locator Slips should be prepared and filled up properly and approved by the Head of Office before leaving the office

We recommended that Management should submit copies of perfected contracts, regardless of amount, within five (5) days from approval/issuance as required under COA Circular 2009-001, together with the complete set of bidding documents which are deemed necessary in the conduct of auditorial review and in the technical evaluation thereof.

We also recommended that the complete bidding documents of the above-mentioned perfected contracts be submitted immediately to avoid the issuance of a Notice of Suspension.

We recommended that Management should process the disbursing voucher/claim on the advance payment/mobilization fee only upon submission of any form of security by the contractors as prescribed in Section 4.2 of the Annex E of IRR-RA 9184. 

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant should ensure that disbursement vouchers are supported with complete documents that are required in COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Engineer to see to it that disbursement vouchers for the payments of progress/final billings of various infrastructure projects are duly and properly supported in accordance with COA Circular 2012-001.

We recommended that Management adhere with the provisions prescribed in the Revised IRR of RA 9184 pertaining to warranty security in order to protect the interest of the government and those of the third parties involved in the execution of the government projects.

Require the Municipal Mayor to follow the provisions of RA 9710 and any other issuances pertaining to the disbursement of GAD Fund.

Require the Mun. Budget Officer and Mun. Accountant not to process disbursement vouchers pertaining to GAD if there is no appropriation and if the programs, projects and activities are not identified in the GAD Plan and Budget for the year and does not clearly address gender issues and concerns.

We recommended that management direct  the Municipal  Accountant to submit to the Office of the Auditor the required supporting documents as listed in Appendix 7 for evaluation within ninety 90 days after receipt of the notice of suspension, , otherwise, the suspension will become a disallowance.

We further, recommended that, henceforth, all disbursements by the Municipality should be supported with complete documentation pursuant to Section 4 (6) of Presidential Decree No. 1445 or the State Auditing Code of the Philippines

We recommended that Management return to the source Agency any unused balance upon submission of the liquidation report so that they may be utilized for priority and urgent projects.  

Management should also exert efforts in identifying and documenting long outstanding balances/excess amounts so that settlement with the source agencies may be facilitated.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should exert effort and install strategies to fast tract settlement or liquidation of cash advances thru the following remedial measures:
a) Payment of salaries of concerned officials/employees should not be paid unless cash advances are fully settled.

We recommended that Management direct the heads of the different implementing offices to fully implement projects funded out of the 20% Local Development Fund during the year in order to promptly provide the constituents the benefits due them. observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant shall book up the value of the building razed by fire and the same shall be included in the Report of Inventory of Property.  Management shall prepare justification citing among others the reason for the failure to book up and the delay in filing the request for a relief from property accountability.

Further, we recommended that together with the justification, the requirements set forth in Chapter 4, COA Handbook on Property and Supply Management System shall strictly be followed and attached to the request,  such as: 
 Affidavit executed by the accountable officer stating the following facts: 
Property lost and its valuation
Actual date in which the absence was first noted
Manner of disappearance
Efforts put forth to save the property thru fire
Provisions made to safeguard the property; and
Date when fire was reported to auditor and the police authorities
Joint affidavit of two (2) disinterested persons cognizant of the facts and circumstances about the fire.
Final police report showing the steps taken by the police authorities to safeguard the property from the fire and the present status of the case;
Comments and/or recommendation of the agency head

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Head of the Agency shall ensure strict compliance with Section 262 of GAAM, Section 4 (2) of PD 1445, Section 335 of RA 7160 and Section 43 of General Appropriation Act (GAA) FY 2013. Further, the Municipal Accountant should stop the practice of deducting from employees’ payroll the payment of loans to private banks pursuant to the above-cited rules and regulations. Should the payroll deduction scheme cannot be avoided for any reason, the Municipality should require reasonable fee from the different banks concerned for the collection services rendered to them by the Municipality at the expense of the government. Such service fee should be accounted for by the Municipality as income.
